channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to opt out of airport facial recognition during Thanksgiving travel
item.description: This Thanksgiving, 5.84 million people will descend upon America’s airports, en route to reunite with their loved ones. Many of these travelers may be asked for a facial scan when they go through security.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin is at the doorstep of $100,000 as post-election rally rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin topped $98,000 for the first time Thursday, extending a streak of record after record highs since the U.S. presidential election. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks. Now, bitcoin is at the doorstep of $100,000.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California's Booming Tech Sector Fills State Coffers with Wealth
item.description: California's tech boom fuels state revenue, but concerns arise about sustainability and growing deficits
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: US Justice Department seeks Google Chrome sale to curb monopoly
item.description: The US Justice Department and a group of states proposed major changes to Alphabet Inc.’s Google — including a forced sale of the company’s Chrome web browser — after a landmark ruling that the tech giant illegally monopolized online search.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI agents are coming to work — here’s what businesses need to know
item.description: AI agents will soon be everywhere, automating complex business processes and taking care of mundane tasks for workers — at least that’s the claim of various software vendors that are quickly adding intelligent bots to a wide range of work apps. Many companies appear to be listening.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mastering Advanced Java Generics: A Comprehensive Guide
item.description: Learn about bounded type parameters, wildcards, type erasure, generic methods, multiple bounds, lower bounds, and upper bounds in advanced Java generics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot incorpora nuevos agentes de IA y automatiza tareas para mejorar flujos de trabajo
item.description: El gigante de Redmond, aprovechando el altavoz de su cita anual Microsoft Ignite 2024, ha anunciado nuevas funcionalidades en su cartera de soluciones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google unveils unique and eco-friendly mass timber Sunnyvale office
item.description: Google has unveiled its first mass timber office building, a cutting-edge and eco-friendly structure in Sunnyvale.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: What to do when you hate your favorite influencers’ politics
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's disturbing message
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google Docs makeover: How the word processor is getting smarter
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Google Docs is getting smarter. New sub-pages help organize complex documents, AI can assist with editing, and pageless mode improves online reading.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: San Jose office building linked to high-profile firm flops into default
item.description: A big San Jose office building whose principal owner is a high-profile real estate firm has tumbled into a loan default
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: How your browser is spying on you: Hidden dangers lurking behind every click
item.description: Internet browsers are useful, but they can be dangerous. Clicking on malicious links can direct you to dangerous websites that steal information or infect your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GSA Improving Integrated Acquisition Environment for Broader Customer Base
item.description: GSA's Integrated Acquisition Environment is expanding its customer base beyond procurement, with federal workers in grants, agency-specific programs, and legal offices relying on the system's data to make decisions.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft upgrades Copilot Studio agent builder tools
item.description: Microsoft at this week’s Ignite conference unveiled new Copilot Studio features aimed at both expanding the functionality of AI agents created with the application and improving the accuracy of outputs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ChatGPT’s Windows app beats Microsoft Copilot for productivity
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant appears to be transforming into a chatty AI sidekick, and I’ve seen quite a few Copilot users who aren’t happy about it. Thankfully, there’s now another option for anyone interested in using AI purely for productivity — a full-featured ChatGPT app for Windows PCs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An Android-ChromeOS merger might actually make sense now
item.description: Stop me if you’ve heard this one: Google’s got two primary user-facing platforms, Android and ChromeOS — and, if the latest rumors are to be believed, the company may be on the brink of bringing ’em together and combining ’em into a single, streamlined entity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase
item.description: The Performance Accountability Council latest's report on “Trusted Workforce 2.0 said agencies have updated all major policies related to personnel vetting. The post Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Spin 3.0 supports polyglot development using Wasm components
item.description: Fermyon has released Spin 3.0, a major update to its open source developer tool for building serverless WebAssembly applications. The new release introduces polyglot programming to ease development. The update to Fermyon’s Spin framework was introduced November 11.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
